localizacoes.descricao: The fictional nation equivalent to colonial Brazil. In 2030, it's a land of extremes: cutting-edge technology side by side with favelas, armored wealth, and a drifting periphery. Birthplace of the Xakaxi and the stage for Kim's story.

The fictional São Paulo. A chaotic metropolis with corporate towers, dangerous outskirts, and the invisible presence of Yohualticit on every corner. This is where Kim sells candy on buses — and where everything begins.
Bravara's most advanced school. Augmented reality classrooms, AI assistants, facial recognition cameras. Kim and Jack study here on a 50% scholarship — secured with the help of gatekeeper Osvaldo.
A deactivated building on Elite Academy's campus. Kim and Jack's secret refuge — and later the trio's headquarters. House rule: no fighting here. Anyone who risks it loses access.
A nightclub in one of Marelia's most degraded areas. Kim and Jack work night security here. Violent environment, R$300 a night — negotiated up from R$200 by Jack. The 'Big Man' owns the place.
A decommissioned police station turned parking lot, full of rusted impounded cars. Where the trio drinks, talks, and plans. No hierarchy, no cameras. Their confessional booth.
An island in the South Pacific. David Kronos's private property. Home of the LDI World Tournament — held every 4 years with the top 128 players on Earth. A place where prestige and threat coexist.
The fictional Japan of the ISA universe. Home of Shuntaro Chirasu, ranked 998th worldwide in the SDR. He traveled 9 hours to personally challenge Kim at Elite Academy.

timeline.texto: The Xakaxi tribe thrives: carbon fiber, robotic agriculture, Xakaxium metal, six elements mastered. Then Powa — the shaman's son — performs a forbidden ritual. Pajé Yawanari sacrifices his own body to seal the Sangue Primordial. The tribe's technological memory is erased.
Feudal invaders arrive in Bravara. The Xakaxi, stripped of spiritual protection and technological memory, are conquered. Yawanari watches helplessly as a guardian spirit.
Conquistadors massacre and enslave the native populations of Bravara. Yawanari witnesses every death, trapped in the spirit realm.
Rebellion in Bravara — met with mass public executions. More blood for the spirit who cannot intervene.
The first world-scale conflict. Yawanari watches humanity destroy itself for the first time at industrial scale.
Concentration camps. Bombs. Systematic destruction. The guardian spirit witnesses human cruelty reach new heights.
Towers fall. The world's relationship with security and paranoia changes forever. Yawanari no longer understands the species he swore to guard.
Global pandemic reshapes society. Isolation, mass death, economic collapse. The world that emerges is different — more digital, more unequal.
Yohualticit creates LDI. Within one year, it outsells every console ever released. Three billion people. Real pain. Global rankings. A new kind of sport is born.
Short-video platforms arrive and dominate the world. PowWow explodes. Yawanari, condemned to watch humanity, discovers 15-second videos. He finally loses his mind completely.

Kim is 17, sells candy on buses, and studies at Elite Academy on a 50% scholarship. A bet with Thunderbolt throws him into LDI. The Sangue Primordial wakes up. David Kronos notices. Nothing is ever the same.

modos_combate.descricao: Pure combat without weapons. Consumes the least energy while granting superhuman speed and strength. Ideal for reading your opponent before committing resources. Kim uses it as his base before calling the Xakixi.

Player's personal weapon in action. Acquired through random loot packages or rare spontaneous manifestation (like Kim's Xakixi). Each weapon has Normal Mode and Xamã Mode — Xamã is far more powerful but drains energy fast.

Uses the player's elemental affinity. Three durations: Instant (low cost), Medium, and Long (high power, high cost — player is vulnerable during charging). Max 4 active powers per battle. New powers can emerge spontaneously mid-fight.

elementais.intro: Every player connects to an elemental upon registration. There is no power difference between primary and secondary elementals — only origin. Kim's anomaly (registered Fire, manifested Darkness) is technically impossible by LDI's system. Cause: the Sangue Primordial overwriting the code.

ranking_sdr.label: Beginner
ranking_sdr.descricao: Newcomers. No relevance in the scene.

ranking_sdr.label: Low Relevance
ranking_sdr.descricao: Almost no audience. Fights generate no income.

ranking_sdr.label: Some Gain
ranking_sdr.descricao: Starting to appear in PowWow searches.

ranking_sdr.label: 40k Free
ranking_sdr.descricao: Widely watched. Open public matches. Possible income.

ranking_sdr.label: 10k Specials
ranking_sdr.descricao: Real income. Sponsors, donations, and significant bets. Kim entered this tier in a single battle.

ranking_sdr.label: World Tournament
ranking_sdr.descricao: Global elite. Every 4 years on Davoss Island. Wealth, fame — and David Kronos waiting.

xakaxi.intro: In 1450, the Xakaxi were an indigenous civilization centuries ahead of their time. Not primitive — advanced. Until a forbidden ritual changed everything.

tecnologias.descricao: Ultra-resistant material used in weapons and construction. Exclusive to the tribe in that era.
Automated food production systems. The tribe did not depend on manual labor to survive.
Optical devices that enhanced vision for hunting, warfare, and precision craftsmanship.
Metal exclusive to the Xakaxi. Exceptional durability and energy conductivity. Used in weapons and ceremonial items. Vanished after the 1450 sealing.
Decorative metal used in Xakaxi adornments and jewelry. Nara, the matriarch, wore Nexium jewelry.
Fire, Earth, Air, Water, Light, and Darkness — mastered by the Xakaxi as real spiritual powers. They became the foundation of LDI's elemental system, recreated by Yohualticit centuries later.

glossario.descricao: The virtual arena created by Yohualticit in 2022. 100% real pain. Three billion players. The most-watched sport in the world by 2030.
Hardware that connects the nervous system to the MDI. Composed of Immersor (body), Goggles (visual immersion), and Tech Glove (movement translation). Limited editions cost 60–70k units.
The virtual dimension where LDI battles take place. Built by Yohualticit. Inside it, the laws of the real world are partially rewritten.
The physical real world — outside the SBI. This is where Kim sells candy, pays electricity bills, and lives with Helena.
LDI's global ranking system. Classifies every player in the world by performance. Determines income, visibility, and access to the World Tournament.
The dominant short-video social network of 2030, dedicated to LDI. Kim vs. Thunderbolt went top 10 worldwide on the same day.
Bravara's instant digital payment system. The fictional Pix. Helena asks Kim for DIX regularly. Kim and Jack use it to receive their nightclub pay.
LDI's AI assistant. Guides new players through avatar creation and game mechanics. Communicates directly into the player's mind. Auto-generates nicknames for newcomers.
Ancestral power of the Xakaxi sealed by Pajé Yawanari in 1450. Manifests as black flames, white hair, and tribal tattoos. Kim is the current host — and he doesn't control it consciously.
The only physical vessel capable of containing David Kronos's essence. Kim is the only Primordial Body in existence. That's why Kronos hunts him.
Kim's transformed state when Sangue Primordial is active. Appearance: white hair, Xakaxi tattoos, black flames. Personality: predatory, ancient. Temporary — Kim has no memory of the transformations.
Kim's weapon. A pair of tribal hatchets with high-tech handles and a retractable steel cable. Spontaneous manifestation — not purchased. Xamã Mode: merges into a throwable double-bladed axe.
Allows spectators to wager points on LDI battles. The winner receives up to 10% of all points bet. One of the income sources for high-ranking players.
Spectators donate to players during matches. Larger donations can request name mentions, specific moves, or shoutouts — pre-approved by the CLI.
Allows fights to be made private, pay-per-view, or exclusive to paying groups. Used by high-level players to monetize special matchups.
Paid training sessions, classes, and demonstrations of special techniques. Famous players monetize by coaching others.
Connects players to brands and sponsors. Yohu handles everything — the player just receives payment. Avatars and arenas may be modified per partner requirements.
Every 6 months, a player can create 10–30 unique NFTs in partnership with a Yohu designer. Buyers receive rewards and can trade the NFTs on the internal market.
The Xakaxi's exclusive metal from 1450. Exceptional durability and energy conductivity. Used in weapons and ceremonial items. Vanished after the sealing — but Kim's Xakixi uses derived technology.
David Kronos's plan to destroy and renew the world. Requires the Primordial Body — Kim's body — to execute. The final objective of the series' main antagonist.
